parsed_review.summary:
CP00-820 is the RP27 Platform Extensibility scope-and-domain-foundation descriptor pack (risk C, 150 units, RP27.P00.M00.S01-RP27.P01.M05.S11), opening RP27 after CP00-819 closed RP26. Reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob.

Production-ready claim is correctly scoped and honest. The manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status:in_progress, with production_ready_flag="platform_extensibility_scope_domain_foundation_descriptor_verified" and implementation_summary asserting descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, production_ready_descriptor_verified:false. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim; the descriptor-verified flag is the only assertion.

Implementation is consistent and well-bounded, verified independently. registry.js PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP820_PACK_BINDING (150 units, range RP27.P00.M00.S01-RP27.P01.M05.S11, upstream CP00-819, next CP00-821/RP27.P01.M05.S12) matches the baseline, manifest, and live closeout-pack-plan.json (CP00-820 entry has included_units). PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP820_REQUIREMENTS.deliverable_counts {implementation:106, contract:7, security_audit:11, ui:14, fixture:2, test:6, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:2}=150 match my own direct count of the baseline included_units (implementation 106, ui 14, security_audit 11, contract 7, test 6, fixture+hermes+claude 6). phase_counts {RP27.P00:78, RP27.P01:72}, micro_phase_row_counts, and micro_title_row_counts all reconcile to 150. Row constants verified: SCOPE_ROWS=3, CONTRACT_ROWS=11, DOMAIN_ROWS=22, with section slices (M06/M08/M09=CONTRACT_ROWS.slice(0,8)=8, P01.M00=3, P01.M01/M02=8, P01.M03=22, P01.M04=20, P01.M05=11) reproducing the expected counts. model.js models are tenant_scoped, require matter_trace_ref, and reference AuditEvent; states.js enumerates descriptor-suffixed statuses and EXTENSION_RISK_CLAIMS (6, incl. cross_tenant_extension_access); validators.js validateCoverage/validateDescriptor enforce distribution/row drift plus descriptor_only/no-secret/no-real-data guards and runtime-closed assertions; createPlatformExtensibilityContractProjection emits the contract. The contract validates internally (validation.valid:true, latest_pack/plan_pack CP00-820, latest_projection.descriptor=PlatformExtensibilityCp820ScopeDomainFoundationDescriptor, no_write_attestation present), all 9 mandatory_artifacts exist on disk, and required_capabilities/safety_gates/no_leak_guards are complete. No secrets, keys, emails, or real client data found in the contract (only *_included:false attestation flags). model.test.js asserts coverage.errors==[], by_deliverable deepEqual, descriptor runtime-closed, registry tenant-scoped/audit-traceable, and contract projection current; it is wired into the root npm test glob (packages/platform/test/*.test.js).

Authority and CP178+ review-hardening boundaries hold. Claude stays read_only_independent_reviewer (allowed_claude_tools=[Read,Grep,Glob]; claude_is_final_approval:false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false); enterprise-trust closure is external to this receipt and the pack does not claim it; Hermes is descriptor-only control-plane handling (emits_runtime_receipt:false) and is not embedded in product runtime. forbidden_review_evidence is defined (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit_error, permission_denial, write_tool_used, runtime_receipt_claim); readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1435-byte stdout, valid JSON {"ok":true}, no stderr); validation-report confirms summary_only_review_allowed:false (Risk C + security/audit + review-evidence), forbidden_tools_present:false, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted:[]. No prior review receipt exists to evaluate (this is the single pending review).

Provenance: all PASS judgments above are from direct source inspection. As a read-only reviewer I did NOT execute npm test / npm run *:validate / git diff --check; command/test PASS is established by source consistency and the live plan/contract alignment, not fresh execution. Closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md) are not yet present — the expected pre-closeout state, matching the prior closed pack CP00-819. Three P3 informational notes only; no P0/P1/P2. These do not block pack closeout, and the human/enterprise-trust approval boundary being external does not block this CP closeout goal.

findings.title: Closeout artifacts not yet present at review time; validation commands not independently executed (read-only)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-820/ currently contains only manifest.json, and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending_review' with empty valid_review_receipts/invalid_review_attempts. This is the expected pre-closeout state: this review is the gate that precedes generating command-evidence (validation command results), claude-review-result, construction-inspection, and adjudication, and flips production_ready/runtime status, exactly as the prior closed pack CP00-819 demonstrates (it now has all five docs artifacts). As a read-only reviewer I could not run npm run closeout-pack:validate / npm test / npm run validate / npm run weighted:validate / git diff --check etc., so command/test PASS is established by source consistency (registry/model/states/validators/contract/tests internally aligned, contract validation.valid:true, live plan CP00-820 included_units distribution matches requirements) and prior-pack precedent rather than fresh execution.
findings.recommendation: Proceed. At closeout, record exactly one valid pack-level review receipt for this run, then generate command-evidence.json (confirm npm test and the listed validators actually exit 0 on the CP00-820 tree, including node scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs), construction-inspection.json, adjudication.md, and claude-review-result.json before flipping status/production_ready.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists and mislabels new-session-handoff.md
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 144) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 153), and is labeled an implementation file though it is a session-handoff doc unrelated to the platform package. The authoritative pack scope is unaffected: active_pack_implementation_refs lists only the 11 platform files (contracts/platform-extensibility-contract.json, package.json, packages/platform/{README.md,package.json,src/index.js,src/model.js,src/registry.js,src/states.js,src/validators.js,test/model.test.js}, scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs), active_pack_required_untracked_files is docs/closeout-packs/cp00-820, and validation-report confirms active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[] and forbidden_tools_present=false. The manifest worktree_transition_policy also treats new-session-handoff.md as unrelated. Same cosmetic labeling carried over from the prior pack's CP819-R02.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten changed-file-scope classification so a file is not listed in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, and do not classify docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md as an implementation file; no scope or boundary impact.

findings.title: acceptance_risks summary lists 5 risks while README and blocked_claims treat cross-tenant extension access as a 6th
findings.details: program_contract.acceptance_risks (contract line 30; registry.js PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_PROGRAM_CONTRACT.acceptance_risks) lists 5 risks: API over-permission, webhook replay, workflow unsafe mutation, rate limit bypass, extension leak. However packages/platform/README.md (lines 9-14) enumerates 6 acceptance risks including 'cross-tenant extension access', and states.js EXTENSION_RISK_CLAIMS / the descriptor blocked_claims list 6 including cross_tenant_extension_access. The risk is fully covered elsewhere: blocked_claims (6), safety_gates 'enforce tenant scope', and per-row tenant_scope_field guard cross_tenant_extension_access_allowed:false. So this is a descriptive count inconsistency, not a coverage gap, and is plausibly intentional (acceptance_risks mirrors the RP27 plan's 5 named risks while blocked_claims adds cross-tenant as defense-in-depth).
findings.recommendation: Optionally align the human-readable acceptance_risks list with the README and blocked_claims (add 'cross-tenant extension access') or add a short note that blocked_claims is intentionally a superset; no functional or boundary impact.
